lesson_title: 13.12 — Member selection with pointers and references

cards.text: To access members through a pointer, use the {{c1::member selection from pointer}} operator {{c2::->}}
cards.source: 13.12 — Member selection with pointers and references

cards.question: What's the output?
cards.explanation: ptr->id accesses id member through pointer.
cards.source: 13.12 — Member selection with pointers and references

cards.front: What are the two member selection operators in C++?
cards.back:
• . (dot) - for objects and references
  • -> (arrow) - for pointers
cards.source: 13.12 — Member selection with pointers and references

cards.question: Are these equivalent?
cards.explanation: Arrow operator dereferences pointer and accesses member.
cards.source: 13.12 — Member selection with pointers and references

cards.text: ptr->member is equivalent to {{c1::(*ptr).member}}, where parentheses are needed due to {{c2::operator precedence}}.
cards.source: 13.12 — Member selection with pointers and references

cards.front: Which member selection operator do you use with references?
cards.back:
The dot operator (.). References use the same syntax as regular objects.
ref.member
cards.source: 13.12 — Member selection with pointers and references

cards.question: What's the correct syntax?
cards.explanation: References use dot operator, just like objects.
cards.source: 13.12 — Member selection with pointers and references

cards.question: What's wrong?
cards.explanation: Always check pointers before using arrow operator.
cards.source: 13.12 — Member selection with pointers and references

cards.front: Why is the arrow operator (->) useful?
cards.back: Provides cleaner syntax than (*ptr).member, especially with chained member access or complex expressions.
cards.source: 13.12 — Member selection with pointers and references

cards.explanation:
Choose operator based on type:
  • Object/reference: use . (dot)
  • Pointer: use -> (arrow)
Arrow operator is shorthand for dereference-then-access: ptr->m = (*ptr).m
cards.source: 13.12 — Member selection with pointers and references

cards.text: The member selection operator . has {{c1::higher}} precedence than the dereference operator *, which is why {{c2::(*ptr).member}} needs parentheses.
cards.source: 13.12 — Member selection with pointers and references

cards.text: The {{c1::arrow}} operator (->) is shorthand for {{c2::(*ptr).member}}, used with pointers to access members.
cards.source: 13.12 — Member selection with pointers and references

cards.text: References use the {{c1::dot}} operator (.) for member selection, the same as {{c2::objects}}.
cards.source: 13.12 — Member selection with pointers and references

cards.text: The {{c1::arrow}} operator (->) is shorthand for {{c2::(*ptr).member}}, used with pointers to access members.
cards.source: 13.12 — Member selection with pointers and references
